title: malus halleana
intro: Malus halleana, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malus halleana include reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, bark, and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
